message: Jake
Ginny
Gabriela
Sorry to have you come all the way here. Actually,
I found out who the last customer was.
the last customer
I thought about handling it over the phone,
but I don't want to deal with the possibility of being eavesdropped.
Is there a chance we're being wiretapped?
Well, this is New York. And given the type of company this is, anything could happen, right?
So, did you figure anything out?
No, not yet.
I see.
Welcome.
With that look on your face, it seems I can't expect much.

Unlike the last time I came here, there's a tense atmosphere lingering.
Though nothing seems to have changed looks-wise, there's a sense of lifelessness.
I feel that way.
You're not looking well,
and I think it's because you're worried about.
You seem really tired; I wonder if you'll be okay?
By the way,
who was the last customer?
You know, it felt like it was totally customer-oriented.
Totally customer-oriented, huh?
That girl has good looks and knows how to handle men, so she was doing work limited to a few guys. She was a good earner.
So, there’s a possibility it could be someone from that group.
Yeah. I'm worried it might be something like confinement.
Confinement, you say?
Oh, you're looking at people with suspicion.
You know? That girl was quite popular and knew how to handle men.
Ah, yes.
The clients are paying quite a bit every month to us.
When you say a considerable amount, how much are we talking about?
Well...
About three thousand dollars a month, I’d say.
Three thousand dollars a month, huh?
Yep. Though, on top of that,
it seems there were some who were giving extra allowances, so I can't be sure.
S-so everyone is rich, huh?
Well? Some of them are even in debt.
Did they really want to remain clients that badly?
Just like that.
Aren't they in love?
And that debt-ridden guy was the last customer, Tonny Ford.
Tonny!?
Oh, could it be that you know him?
Actually,
with Tonny,
I've encountered them together before.
Oh, no way!
The world is so small.
I wonder if it's just a simple trouble with a customer.
Still, severing someone's wrist is way too violent.
Ah, yeah, that's true.
I feel so tired, like my mind is going numb.
I met Tony Ford.
So, how was it?
I can't really say yet.
What? That's not good. Get it together.
I met Tony, but he seemed kind of off, like he wasn't making much sense and his gaze was weird.
So, he must be doing drugs again.
Drugs?
Ugh, of all times! This is just awful.
I feel so drained.
Maybe I should take some time off.
She's a mysterious girl, apparently an old acquaintance of actress Jennifer.
With Jennifer?
Yeah, it’s a bit surprising, right? But it seems like they aren't that close.
Just between us, it seems like she stole Jennifer's boyfriend.
And because of that, she was apparently quite resented.
When did that happen?
It was before Jennifer became famous.
Isn't it shocking that she had a history with Jennifer?
I think Jennifer might be behind what's happening.
Isn't that a bit of a leap?
Do you really think so?
But,
I've got a feeling I heard about Jennifer from her. It was pretty recent.
That doesn't mean you can just connect the dots though.
That's true, but...
I doubt everything now.
He’s surely alive, right?
He’s more of a stage actor than a director.
But he did win an award for directing, didn't he?
Ah, there’s someone who cried behind the scenes, I see.
Someone cried behind the scenes? Does that mean it was an award he shouldn't have received?
I don't know. Maybe you should ask him about that?
Tonny lives in Greenwich Village. Could you go and check it out?
That's fine, but will you let me in?
I'll give you a call. Just say it’s for my business.
Thank you in advance.
That's right, it really is my business, I'm not lying, right?
Tony might be a good person at heart, but I can't help but doubt him now.
It seems like he's more tired than the last time.
Surely it must be because he's worried about
or maybe,
the whereabouts of the breadwinner is uncertain, and his anxiety as a manager is growing.
Either way, he's definitely fatigued.
After all, there's no way Jennifer would
confine someone over events from years ago.
That's just overthinking.
Did something happen between Tony and
or is it the work of someone completely different?
If I ask Tonny, I should at least know where we parted ways at the end. Anyway, I need to meet him.
I lost a hand.
I'm worried about his safety. I think I need to find him as soon as possible.
It's hard to imagine that I'm in debt and under contract for him.
It’s a rather unimaginable truth.
When they were at the Hotel, it was as if they were lovers, but was that really built on a contract?
Tonny is
unlikely to be confining him, but there’s always a chance.
Yet, it seems impossible that he would
cut his wrist.
But what do you think?
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
